extraction.one_line_summary: SOLAIRE Marc attacked solo on the finishing circuit loop with 26 seconds gap, holding off the peloton by 22 seconds
tactical_patterns: KUNG Stefan double-attacked on circuit climb but cracked, showing even strong TT riders can't sustain repeated accelerations on rolling circuits.
SOLAIRE's winning move came after early breakaway was neutralized, proving post-catch counterattacks exploit peloton's momentary disorganization better than original breaks.
Movistar deployed SOLAIRE alone without team support on finishing circuits, prioritizing individual opportunism over controlled leadout on technical wet loops.

rider_signals.SOLAIRE Marc: positive: executed perfectly timed solo attack on circuit when peloton caught break, suggesting exceptional race-reading ability in messy conditions
rider_signals.KUNG Stefan: negative: launched double attack on circuit climb but immediately cracked, showing poor form despite TT pedigree
rider_signals.HOEYENS Kobe: neutral: bridged to KUNG initially but couldn't sustain, confirming limited climbing capacity even on moderate gradients
rider_signals.BISIG Stefan: negative: dropped from early break on circuit climbs, indicating insufficient form for even moderate selective terrain
